products.title: Dragon Professional 16
products.metaTitle: Dragon Professional 16 Australia | Speech Recognition for Windows | VRA
products.metaDesc: Dragon Professional 16 for Windows - dictate faster than typing, transcribe audio, automate workflows. Perpetual licence. Offline capable. Works with Word, Outlook, 100+ apps.
products.headline: Dragon Professional 16 - The Professional's Speech Recognition
products.subheadline: Dictate faster than you type. Transcribe recordings. Automate repetitive tasks by voice. Full-featured professional speech recognition for Windows that works offline after activation.
products.licenceNote: Licensed per user. Install on up to 2 Windows PCs.
features: Real-time dictation into Word, Outlook, and 100+ Windows applications
Transcribe single-speaker audio recordings (MP3, WAV, WMA, DS2, DSS)
Custom vocabulary - add industry terms, company names, and medical specialties
AutoText - speak a short phrase to insert an entire paragraph or template
Voice macros to automate multi-step Windows workflows
Works offline after installation and activation
Hands-free PC control, navigation, and application launching
Windows 10 and Windows 11 compatible (32-bit and 64-bit)
Compatible with Microsoft 365, Google Docs, and professional applications

faqs.q: Does Dragon Professional 16 require an internet connection?
faqs.a: No. After installation and activation, Dragon Professional 16 works offline. An internet connection is only required for the initial download and product activation.

faqs.q: How many computers can I use Dragon on?
faqs.a: Dragon Professional 16 can be installed on up to 2 Windows computers under a single licence.

faqs.q: Can I transcribe multi-speaker recordings or meetings?
faqs.a: Dragon Professional 16 is designed for single-speaker audio only. It will not reliably transcribe meetings, interviews, or multi-speaker conversations. Supported formats include MP3, WAV, WMA, DS2, and DSS.

faqs.q: Is this a subscription or permanent purchase?
faqs.a: This is a perpetual licence - a one-time purchase. You own it permanently with no annual renewal fees or subscription charges.

products.metaDesc: Best speech recognition software in the browser - free tier, 57 languages, no install. Professional and medical tiers for Australian businesses and healthcare teams.
products.headline: SpeechRecognition.cloud - Best Free Speech Recognition Software
products.subheadline: Browser-based speech recognition powered by AI. No software to install. 57 languages supported. Free tier available. The best speech to text software for individuals and teams who want to get started without upfront cost.
products.priceNote: Free tier available - professional and medical subscriptions
products.licenceNote: Cloud-based. No installation required. Pay-as-you-go or subscription options.
features: Browser-based - works on any device with a web browser
57 languages supported - global and regional dialects
Free tier for individuals and teams to get started
Professional tier - higher accuracy, priority support, API access
Medical tier - healthcare vocabulary, privacy controls, compliance features
Real-time dictation and audio file transcription
No local software installation - instant access
Cloud storage of transcriptions with privacy controls
Team collaboration and sharing features

faqs.q: Do I need to install software?
faqs.a: No. SpeechRecognition.cloud is entirely browser-based. Just log in from any device and start dictating or uploading audio files.

faqs.q: Is there a free option to try?
faqs.a: Yes. A free tier is available with usage limits. Upgrade to Professional or Medical tier for higher usage allowances and priority support.

faqs.q: How many languages are supported?
faqs.a: 57 languages are supported including major languages, regional dialects, and specialized medical and legal terminology.

faqs.q: Is the Medical tier suitable for clinical use?
faqs.a: The Medical tier includes healthcare-specific vocabulary, enhanced privacy controls, and compliance features suitable for healthcare environments. Consult the platform for specific compliance certifications.

products.title: Dragon Medical One
products.metaTitle: Dragon Medical One Australia | AI Clinical Documentation | VRA
products.metaDesc: Dragon Medical One - cloud-based AI clinical documentation for Australian doctors. Ambient AI for patient notes. HIPAA-aligned. Medical terminology built-in. Contact Voice Recognition Australia.
products.headline: Dragon Medical One - AI-Powered Clinical Documentation
products.subheadline: Cloud-based speech recognition designed for healthcare. Ambient AI captures consultations and generates structured clinical notes. Reduces documentation burden and after-hours work for clinicians.
products.priceNote: Annual subscription - contact for Australian pricing
products.licenceNote: Cloud-based subscription. No local installation required. Access from any device.
features: Ambient AI - captures consultation audio and generates structured clinical notes
Built-in medical vocabulary - cardiology, radiology, pathology, and 40+ specialties
Integrates with major Australian practice management systems
HIPAA-aligned for healthcare data security and privacy
Cloud-based - accessible from any device, no desktop software needed
Automatic transcription and note structuring (SOAP format)
Supports dictation in real-time or from voice recordings
Reduces clinical documentation time and after-hours work
Annual subscription model - professional and enterprise tiers

faqs.q: Is Dragon Medical One available in Australia?
faqs.a: Yes. Dragon Medical One is available through Voice Recognition Australia, an authorised Nuance/Microsoft reseller. It is a cloud-based subscription product designed specifically for healthcare documentation.

faqs.q: What is ambient AI and how does it work?
faqs.a: Ambient AI captures consultation audio and automatically generates structured clinical notes without the clinician having to actively dictate or interact with software. This reduces documentation burden and allows clinicians to focus on patient care.

faqs.q: Do I need local software installation?
faqs.a: No. Dragon Medical One is cloud-based. You access it through a web browser on any device - no desktop software installation required.

faqs.q: What practice management systems does it integrate with?
faqs.a: Dragon Medical One integrates with leading Australian practice systems. Contact Voice Recognition Australia for specific integration compatibility with your system.

products.title: Philips SpeechLive
products.metaTitle: Philips SpeechLive Australia | Cloud Dictation Workflow | VRA
products.metaDesc: Philips SpeechLive cloud dictation workflow for Australian healthcare and legal professionals. Dictate from any device, route to transcription, integrate with Dragon. Authorised reseller.
products.headline: Philips SpeechLive - Cloud Dictation Workflow
products.subheadline: Dictate on any device and route audio securely to your transcriptionist or speech recognition engine. SpeechLive connects Philips hardware, Dragon speech recognition, and your document workflow in one managed cloud platform.
products.priceNote: Annual subscription - contact for Australian pricing
products.licenceNote: Cloud-based subscription. Works with Philips SpeechMike, smartphones, and web browser.
features: Cloud-based dictation - record on any device, anywhere
Integrates with Dragon speech recognition for automated transcription
Route dictations to transcriptionists with workflow rules and priority settings
Secure cloud storage - data encrypted in transit and at rest
Regional data storage on Azure - relevant for Australian privacy requirements
Compatible with Philips SpeechMike, SpeechOne, and smartphone app
iManage and document management system integrations
Web-based transcription interface for typists - no local software needed
Detailed audit trail and workflow reporting

faqs.q: What is Philips SpeechLive?
faqs.a: SpeechLive is a cloud-based dictation workflow platform from Philips. It lets you dictate on any device - phone, SpeechMike, or browser - and securely routes recordings to transcriptionists or speech recognition software for completion.

faqs.q: Does SpeechLive work with Dragon speech recognition?
faqs.a: Yes. Philips SpeechLive integrates directly with Nuance Dragon speech recognition, allowing organisations to route dictations for automated transcription rather than manual typing.

faqs.q: Is SpeechLive suitable for healthcare environments?
faqs.a: Yes. SpeechLive stores data on Microsoft Azure with regional options, publishes security and privacy documentation, and is widely used in medical practices for clinical documentation workflows.

faqs.q: Can I use SpeechLive with my existing Philips hardware?
faqs.a: Yes. SpeechLive is compatible with the Philips SpeechMike range, SpeechOne wireless microphone, and the DataVoice smartphone app. Existing Philips hardware users can migrate to the cloud workflow without replacing equipment.

products.title: Dragon Professional Anywhere
products.metaTitle: Dragon Professional Anywhere Australia | Cloud Speech Recognition | VRA
products.metaDesc: Dragon Professional Anywhere - cloud-based speech recognition for Windows and Mac. Dictate from any device. No local installation. Subscription licensing. Authorised Australian reseller.
products.headline: Dragon Professional Anywhere - Speech Recognition in the Cloud
products.subheadline: The full power of Dragon Professional available in the cloud. Dictate from any Windows or Mac device without installing local software. Cloud-based subscription model.
products.licenceNote: Cloud-based subscription. Unlimited device access. Managed via web account.
features: Full Dragon Professional 16 features in the cloud
Works on Windows and Mac - no platform limitations
Install on unlimited devices under subscription
Requires internet connection for use
Custom vocabulary and user profiles in the cloud
Deep integration with Microsoft 365, Google Docs, and web applications
Enterprise deployment options available
Annual subscription with flexible renewal options
Centralised user management for team deployments

faqs.q: Do I need internet to use Dragon Professional Anywhere?
faqs.a: Yes. Dragon Professional Anywhere is cloud-based and requires an active internet connection to use.

faqs.q: Can I use it on Mac and Windows?
faqs.a: Yes. Dragon Professional Anywhere works on both Windows and Mac computers.

faqs.q: How many devices can I install on?
faqs.a: With the subscription, you can install on unlimited devices under your account. However, only one concurrent session is typically allowed - check your licence terms.

faqs.q: What's the difference between Dragon Professional 16 and Dragon Professional Anywhere?
faqs.a: Dragon Professional 16 is locally installed for Windows only and works offline. Dragon Professional Anywhere is cloud-based, works on Windows and Mac, requires internet, and is subscription-based.

products.headline: Professional Dictation Hardware
products.subheadline: Complete dictation equipment - digital voice recorders, professional microphones, foot pedals, and headsets from Philips and Olympus. Healthcare and legal-grade security features.
products.priceNote: Foot pedals - Recorders and mics priced individually
products.licenceNote: One-off hardware purchase. Local Australian stock and support available.
features: Philips digital voice recorders - DS series with encryption and cloud sync
Olympus dictation recorders - professional models with premium audio quality
Professional USB and wireless microphones for desktop dictation
SpeechMike microphones - optimised for Dragon Professional use

Professional headsets and noise-cancelling microphones

Local Australian stock and technical support

faqs.q: What dictation recorder should I use for healthcare?
faqs.a: Philips SpeechMike or SpeechOne devices are widely used with Dragon Medical One. Olympus DS series recorders are popular for clinicians who prefer remote dictation. The choice depends on your EMR integration and workflow.

faqs.q: Can I use a regular microphone with Dragon Professional?
faqs.a: Yes, most USB microphones work with Dragon Professional. However, dedicated dictation microphones like SpeechMike are optimised for speech recognition and typically offer better accuracy.

faqs.q: What is a foot pedal used for?
faqs.a: A foot pedal provides hands-free control of audio playback during transcription - play, pause, rewind, and fast-forward. This allows typists and transcriptionists to keep both hands on the keyboard.

faqs.q: Do you have professional recorders with encryption?
faqs.a: Yes. Philips DS series and Olympus professional models include 256-bit AES encryption for healthcare and legal environments requiring data security.

faqs.q: How long does transcription take?

faqs.q: Are transcripts confidential?

faqs.q: What file formats do you accept?
faqs.a: We accept MP3, WAV, WMA, M4A, and most common audio formats. Video files can also be processed. Contact us with your specific format.

faqs.q: Do you provide specialist medical or legal transcription?

faqs.q: What is the difference between Dragon and a dictation recorder?
faqs.a: Dragon software converts your speech to text in real time as you dictate into a computer or microphone. A digital dictation recorder captures your voice as an audio file, which is then transcribed later - either manually by a typist or via software. Many professionals use both: a recorder in the field, Dragon at the desk.

faqs.q: Do I need Dragon software or is cloud speech recognition enough?
faqs.a: It depends on your workflow. Dragon Professional works offline, has deep Windows application integration, and can automate complex tasks by voice. speech to text software from SpeechRecognition.cloud is a lightweight installed Windows app that works in any application - the base package is free and it is a cost-effective way to get started. We can help you work out which approach suits your situation.

faqs.q: What dictation hardware do you recommend for healthcare?
faqs.a: For healthcare workflows, Philips SpeechMike and SpeechOne devices are widely used with Dragon Medical One. Olympus digital recorders work well for clinicians who prefer to dictate remotely and have audio transcribed. The right choice depends on whether you are dictating directly into an EMR or using a separate transcription workflow.

faqs.q: Is Dragon Medical One available in Australia?
faqs.a: Yes. Dragon Medical One is available through Voice Recognition Australia, Australia's number one Nuance/Microsoft reseller for speech recognition and Dragon software. VRA's founder and CEO Russell Bewsell has 28 years of experience in the speech recognition industry and is one of Australia's leading authorities on Dragon deployment in healthcare and enterprise. Contact Voice Recognition Australia for current Australian pricing.

faqs.q: What is a foot pedal used for in dictation?
faqs.a: A foot pedal lets a typist or transcriptionist control audio playback hands-free during transcription - play, pause, rewind and fast-forward using their feet while their hands remain on the keyboard. Philips and Olympus both make foot pedals designed for their respective dictation workflows.

faqs.q: What is Philips SpeechLive and who is it for?
faqs.a: Philips SpeechLive is a cloud-based dictation workflow platform. You dictate on any device - phone, SpeechMike, or web browser - and recordings are securely routed to a transcriptionist or to Dragon speech recognition for automated conversion. It is widely used in healthcare, legal, and professional services environments that need a managed, auditable dictation workflow.
